text: This statement is false. If the statement is true, then what it says must hold: it is false. But if it is false, then what it says does not hold, so it must be true. Therefore the statement is both true and false.

It is always wrong to use a person merely as a means to an end. Redirecting a runaway trolley to kill one person in order to save five uses that one person as a means. Therefore it is wrong to redirect the trolley. Yet allowing five people to die when you could prevent it seems equally wrong.
The chronicle says the treaty was signed in 1648, yet another chapter says the war ended in 1649, and both claims are attributed to one archive.
